title: User Datagram Protocol
description: Connectionless, fast, unreliable transport protocol with minimal overhead.
fullExplanation: UDP sends datagrams without establishing a connection, without guaranteeing delivery, without ordering, and without retransmission. Its header is only 8 bytes. This makes it extremely fast and suitable for applications where speed matters more than reliability: DNS lookups, VoIP, video streaming, online gaming, and DHCP. Applications that need reliability on top of UDP implement it themselves (e.g., QUIC, RTP with RTCP).

fields.name: Length
fields.description: Length of UDP header + data in bytes

keyFacts: Header is only 8 bytes vs TCP's 20+ bytes
No connection setup — data sent immediately
No retransmission — lost packets are gone
No ordering — packets may arrive out of order
DNS uses UDP port 53 (TCP for responses >512 bytes)
UDP amplification: small request → large response (DDoS vector)
QUIC builds reliability on top of UDP
RTP (Real-time Transport Protocol) uses UDP for VoIP/video
